subject: Group portrait at the Műjégpálya artificial ice skating rink, Városliget (Budapest XIV district), 1938. Vajdahunyad Castle visible in background.
context_for_lesson: A 1938 group photograph at the Műjégpálya, Budapest's famous artificial ice rink in Városliget — the very 14th district of Budapest where Judy Abrams's family lived on Stefánia út. The Műjégpálya was (and remains) Europe's largest open-air ice rink, opened in 1870. The photograph captures the easy, ordinary leisure of pre-war Budapest in Judy's own neighborhood, one year after her birth and six years before her family had to send her into hiding.

lesson_use_intent: Page 2 (Meet Judy) or Page 3 (Before We Read). Caption: 'A group at the Műjégpálya ice rink, Budapest, 1938 — Városliget park, in the same district where Judy lived. Hungarian children skated here through the 1930s and 1940s, growing up in a city that would soon be transformed by war.'
concerns: Strong photo. Same Budapest neighborhood as Judy's home (XIV district). Caption should make the geographic connection without claiming Judy is in the photo.
